hints.junior: The console snippet is your fastest tool: paste it in DevTools and it logs every element whose right edge goes past the viewport.
hints.mid: Don't add overflow-x: hidden to html or body — it silently breaks position: sticky on every element inside. Fix the source element instead.
hints.staff: Horizontal overflow in production is often introduced by a new image or SVG without max-width: 100%. Add this to your CI visual regression checks.

hints.junior: See how one wide element creates a horizontal scrollbar for the whole page? That's why finding the culprit is more important than hiding the overflow.
hints.mid: min-width: 0 is the fix for flex children that overflow. The default min-width: auto prevents flex items from shrinking below their content width.
hints.staff: The fixed demo uses both max-width: 100% and min-width: 0 — these two properties address the two most common overflow mechanisms.

anecdotes.junior: I once spent 30 minutes hunting a horizontal scrollbar on mobile. It was a 600px-wide SVG in a marketing section that had no viewBox. The console snippet found it in 3 seconds.
anecdotes.mid: overflow-x: hidden on body is the most copied StackOverflow 'fix' — and it breaks sticky headers on the same page. The console snippet is always the right first step.
anecdotes.staff: Min-width: 0 on flex children is the most commonly missing CSS in component library audits. It's a spec quirk that surprises even experienced CSS authors.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're hunting down that annoying horizontal scrollbar that shows up on mobile. One JS snippet will find the culprit in seconds.
sections.mid: This case covers horizontal overflow — the console debugging snippet, why overflow-x: hidden on body breaks sticky, and the CSS defaults that prevent this.
sections.staff: We're covering the full overflow debugging toolkit: getBoundingClientRect audit, min-width: 0 for flex, and CI integration to catch this before it ships.
